relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Some will see the Divine reign come in power.
On the high mountain, Jesus is transfigured before the inner three.
His garments blaze with impossible whiteness.
Moses and Elijah appear in conversation.
Peter tries to house the glory in three tents.
Fear speaks before understanding.
The cloud names the beloved Son and commands listening.
Then only Jesus remains before them.
The vision is held until rising from the dead.
They keep the word but do not yet understand rising.
They ask about Elijah coming first.
Elijah restores, yet the Son of Humanity must suffer.
The Elijah-pattern has already been treated according to human will.
They descend into argument and crowd-pressure.
The crowd runs to greet Jesus.
Jesus asks what the argument is about.
A father brings his son whose speech is bound.
The disciples could not bring release.
Jesus grieves the untrusting generation and calls the child near.
The affliction reacts as the child is brought to Jesus.
Jesus asks the story of the suffering.
The father asks compassion and help.
Jesus turns the question toward trust.
The father cries the honest prayer: I trust; help my untrust.
Jesus commands the speechless and deaf spirit to leave and not return.
The release looks like death before restoration.
Jesus takes him by the hand and raises him.
Privately, the disciples ask why they could not release him.
Jesus points them toward prayer, with fasting noted in some manuscripts.
They pass through Galilee hidden from public attention.
He teaches the handing-over, death, and rising of the Son of Humanity.
They do not understand and are afraid to ask.
In the house, he asks about their argument on the way.
They are silent because they argued about greatness.
Firstness becomes lastness and service of all.
A child is placed in the center and held.
Receiving the child receives Jesus and the Sender.
John worries about someone outside their group acting in the name.
Jesus refuses possessive control of the work.
Not against us is for us.
Even a cup of water in the name is remembered.
Causing little ones to stumble carries severe warning.
Whatever causes stumbling must be treated radically.
A later manuscript repeats Isaiah’s warning image.
Even the foot must yield if it blocks life.
The variant warning repeats.
The eye must yield if it blocks the reign.
Gehenna imagery draws from unquenched prophetic warning.
Everyone is salted with fire.
Salt and peace belong together inside the community.
